_comment: v3.1 narration: added 7 internal-campaign sections (08-objection through 14-adoption-cta). Legacy 08-pilot and 09-signoff audio keys are preserved (no Birdie regen needed). New campaign keys: 08-objection, 09-inside-hero, 10-cast, 11-drops, 12-calendar, 13-distribution, 14-adoption-cta — those are the 7 new MP3s Birdie should generate. Voice and settings unchanged.

sections.text: Every quarter, we leave revenue on the table that we never knew was there. Not because our sellers aren't good — they are. It's because the signal got buried before they could see it. So... this is a proposal to fix that.

Two-thirds of an A.E.'s week isn't selling. It's hunting. Across systems, across spreadsheets, across the gaps between them. The intelligence is in there somewhere... it just isn't reaching the seller in time to matter.

What changes the math is one platform underneath all of it. Same data — different surface for each seat. The seller sees the next move on their account. The manager sees the leading indicators across the team. The executive sees the posture of the whole region. One source of truth... three lenses.

Here's what AMP Signal actually is. It's the intelligence, focus, and clarity of a 20-year media veteran — with a team of analysts working around the clock — handed to every seller, in every market, every day. Six modules. Bespoke per role. Powered by specialized agents that bring the work to the seller, instead of making the seller go find it. And it's not a deck. It's a working POC. Already running.

This is where the platform earns its keep. The renewal conversation isn't a renewal anymore — it's an expansion. The pacing data, the upsell logic — already there before the meeting starts. The seller walks in... and the room shifts.
Every meeting our sellers take begins one of two ways. "Tell me about your business." Or — "we know your business." Amp Signal makes the second one the default.

Sinclair's political franchise is one of the most valuable assets in our portfolio. And right now? Planning around it lives in spreadsheets and tribal knowledge. Amp Signal puts it on rails — every market, every cycle, two years out.

I'm being deliberately conservative here. Three independent buckets, ten percent each. Even if you don't believe one of them... the math still clears the bar. We won't know the real numbers until we run the pilot. That's the point of running it.

Here's the part nobody wants to admit. Every Sinclair tool rollout you've seen end the same way — installed, trained on, ignored. Adoption hasn't been a software problem. It's been a people problem. About seventy percent of enterprise software rollouts fail to hit adoption goals. So this time — we built the adoption first.

This is Inside the Signal. A twelve-week internal marketing campaign — already architected. It does two jobs at once. Right now... it's the proof to you that adoption isn't an afterthought. After greenlight... it becomes the engine that drives the actual adoption. Real characters. Real cadence. Real plan. What follows is the actual plan.

Five recurring characters carry the whole campaign. Maya — the early believer. Marcus — the burned-by-rollouts skeptic who comes around. Diane — the regional sales manager whose endorsement opens doors. Me — the pitchman, host. And the dashboard itself — the platform treated as a recurring character. The converted skeptic does more for adoption than the early evangelist ever could.

Twelve reusable drop formats. Five hero pieces — serious, executive-credible, paced through the quarter. Seven shorter playful drops — Slack-native, designed to spread, never overwhelming. Roughly sixty-forty static-to-motion. We've already produced three of them as proof — the launch film, the tab count, the A.E. diary. You can see them now.

Twenty-six drops across twelve weeks. About two pieces per week. Three phases — six weeks of teaser before launch, twelve in-pilot, then expansion to the rest of the regions. Every week is named. Every drop is mapped. No filler.

Internal marketing fails when it asks people to check a new inbox. So we don't. The drops live where attention already lives — Slack, email, all-hands, intranet, regional meetings, onboarding. Embedded, not added. Each channel gets the format that fits how it's consumed.

Adoption isn't a hope. It's a plan with a calendar. Inside the Signal is real — already produced, already scheduled, already in motion. The first drop is live. Open it in a new tab. Judge it for yourself. That's the only honest test.

One region. Twelve weeks. A small embedded pod. Bounded enough that the bet is small — long enough that the evidence is real. We come back with modeled-versus-actual on every claim in this deck. And then... you decide whether we scale it, or whether we stop.
See it first. Win it first.
Something is coming.   It changes the way you sell.   ...Unless you like wasting time.
